---
title: "E5.1 — Conceptual-similarity dendrogram of justice concepts"
subtitle: "habilitace-1; result for chapter 2.7 (method in Appendix E)"
date: "2026-07-11"
format: { html: { toc: true } }
execute: { warning: false, message: false }
---

<!--
  E5.1 per Appendix E. Inputs: three cross-provider coder matrices
  (typology_matrix_{opus,openai,glm}.csv). Reliability across providers -> consensus ->
  Gower distance -> NeighborNet + Ward exploratory similarity maps; no a priori families.
  Human-validation and the character-free convergence check are appended by the
  dedicated scripts after the core manifest is written, so a full run ends with
  all three evidence blocks in one frozen manifest.
  Run from analyzy/: Rscript -e 'knitr::purl("notebooks/50_typology.qmd","/tmp/50.R",quiet=TRUE); source("/tmp/50.R")'
-->

```{r setup}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr); library(tidyr); library(cluster); library(phangorn)
  library(ape); library(irr)
})
set.seed(744)
find_root <- function(){ for(p in c(".","..","../..")) if(dir.exists(file.path(p,"data/processed"))) return(normalizePath(p)); stop("root") }
ROOT<-find_root(); PROC<-file.path(ROOT,"data","processed"); TAB<-file.path(ROOT,"vystupy","tabulky"); FIG<-file.path(ROOT,"vystupy","obrazky")
MAN<-list(); put<-function(k,v){ MAN[[k]]<<-v; invisible(v) }

coders <- c(opus="opus", openai="openai", glm="glm")
mats <- lapply(coders, function(c) read.csv(file.path(PROC, paste0("typology_matrix_",c,".csv")), stringsAsFactors=FALSE))
taxa <- read.csv(file.path(PROC,"typology_taxa.csv"), stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
CH <- unique(mats[[1]]$character)                       # 16 chars in order
stopifnot(length(CH)==16)
put("n_taxa", nrow(taxa)); put("n_characters", 16); put("n_coders", 3)
put("coders", "opus (Anthropic) / gpt-5.5 (OpenAI) / glm-5.2 (Zhipu@e-INFRA)")
```

## Reliability across providers (Krippendorff's alpha)

```{r alpha}
# unit = taxon_id + char_id; build a 3 x 384 matrix of codes (rows=coders)
key <- function(m) paste(m$taxon_id, m$char_id, sep="_")
units <- key(mats[[1]])
codemat <- sapply(mats, function(m){ m<-m[match(units, key(m)),]; m$code })
codemat <- t(codemat)                                   # 3 coders x 384 units
rownames(codemat) <- names(coders)
# Legacy pooled diagnostic across all feature cells. Because the 16 features have different
# permitted state spaces, this value is retained for audit continuity but is NOT interpreted
# in the book; feature-specific alphas below are the reporting basis.
lev <- unique(as.vector(codemat)); intmat <- matrix(match(codemat, lev), nrow=3, dimnames=dimnames(codemat))
a_overall <- irr::kripp.alpha(intmat, method="nominal")$value
put("kripp_alpha_overall", round(a_overall,3))
put("kripp_alpha_overall_note", "legacy pooled diagnostic across heterogeneous feature state spaces; not interpreted; use E5_alpha_per_character.csv")

# per-character alpha
char_of <- mats[[1]]$character[match(units, key(mats[[1]]))]
per <- sapply(CH, function(cc){
  sub <- intmat[, char_of==cc, drop=FALSE]
  tryCatch(round(irr::kripp.alpha(sub, method="nominal")$value,3), error=function(e) NA)
})
per_df <- data.frame(character=CH, alpha=as.numeric(per))
write.csv(per_df, file.path(TAB,"E5_alpha_per_character.csv"), row.names=FALSE)
put("kripp_alpha_char_min", round(min(per,na.rm=TRUE),3))
put("kripp_alpha_char_below_.667", sum(per < .667, na.rm=TRUE))
cat("Krippendorff alpha overall =", round(a_overall,3), "\n"); print(per_df)
```

## Consensus matrix (majority of the three coders)

```{r consensus}
# per unit: majority token; if all three differ, tie-break to opus (documented)
consensus <- sapply(seq_along(units), function(j){
  v <- codemat[,j]; tb <- sort(table(v), decreasing=TRUE)
  if(length(tb)>=1 && tb[1] >= 2) names(tb)[1] else v["opus"]
})
cons <- data.frame(taxon_id=mats[[1]]$taxon_id, character=char_of, code=consensus, stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
wide <- cons |> pivot_wider(names_from=character, values_from=code) |> as.data.frame()
rownames(wide) <- taxa$taxon[match(wide$taxon_id, taxa$id)]
W <- wide[, CH]; W[] <- lapply(W, factor)
put("consensus_ties_allthree_differ", sum(apply(codemat,2,function(v) length(unique(v))==3)))
write.csv(cbind(taxon=rownames(wide), wide[,CH]), file.path(TAB,"E5_consensus_matrix.csv"), row.names=FALSE)
```

## Exploratory similarity map: Gower distance -> NeighborNet + Ward

```{r trees}
D <- cluster::daisy(W, metric="gower")

# NeighborNet (reticulate network) — auxiliary exploratory map, with no family encoding
nn <- tryCatch(phangorn::neighborNet(D), error=function(e){message("nnet: ",conditionMessage(e)); NULL})
if(!is.null(nn)){
  draw <- function() tryCatch(plot(nn, type="2D", tip.color="black", cex=0.7),
                              error=function(e) plot(nn, tip.color="black", cex=0.7))
  png(file.path(FIG,"E5_dendrogram.png"), width=3000, height=2200, res=230); draw(); dev.off()
  pdf(file.path(FIG,"E5_dendrogram.pdf"), width=12, height=9); draw(); dev.off()
}

# Ward dendrogram + simple data-based diagnostic for a display cut.
# With only 24 concepts this selects an exploratory visual aid, not a validated typology.
hc <- hclust(D, method="ward.D2")
ks <- 2:8
diag <- data.frame(
  k=ks,
  average_silhouette=vapply(ks, function(k)
    mean(cluster::silhouette(cutree(hc,k), D)[,"sil_width"]), numeric(1))
)
write.csv(diag, file.path(TAB,"E5_cluster_diagnostics.csv"), row.names=FALSE)
best_k <- diag$k[which.max(diag$average_silhouette)]
runner <- diag[order(diag$average_silhouette, decreasing=TRUE),][2,]
put("cluster_selection_method", "maximum average silhouette over k=2..8; exploratory because n=24")
put("cluster_k_selected", best_k)
put("cluster_silhouette_selected", round(max(diag$average_silhouette),3))
put("cluster_silhouette_runner_up", round(runner$average_silhouette,3))
put("cluster_silhouette_delta", round(max(diag$average_silhouette)-runner$average_silhouette,3))
cl <- cutree(hc, best_k)
measured <- rownames(wide) %in% c("Procedural justice","Equality","Need","Merit/desert (equity)")
write.csv(data.frame(taxon=rownames(wide), ward_cluster=unname(cl), measured_in_book=measured),
          file.path(TAB,"E5_cluster_membership.csv"), row.names=FALSE)
cat("Exploratory silhouette-selected cut: k =",best_k,"\n"); print(diag)

# Contested (low-alpha) characters are NOT discarded. Three views are reported side by side:
#   A) all 16, equal weights  (primary D above)   B) reliable-9 subset   C) alpha-weighted
# (b) reliable-9 subset — binary drop, sensitivity only
rel <- per_df$character[!is.na(per_df$alpha) & per_df$alpha >= .667]
put("n_reliable_chars", length(rel))
put("dropped_chars_low_alpha", paste(setdiff(CH, rel), collapse="; "))
if(length(rel) >= 3){
  hc_rel <- hclust(cluster::daisy(W[,rel,drop=FALSE], metric="gower"),"ward.D2")
  put("cophenetic_r_all_vs_reliable", round(cor(cophenetic(hc),cophenetic(hc_rel)),3))
}
# (c) alpha-weighted Gower — nothing dropped; each character contributes in proportion to its
#     inter-coder reliability (negative/NA alpha clamped to 0). The principled middle ground.
wts <- pmax(0, per_df$alpha[match(CH, per_df$character)]); wts[is.na(wts)] <- 0
Dw <- cluster::daisy(W, metric="gower", weights=wts)
hc_w <- hclust(Dw, "ward.D2")
put("cophenetic_r_all_vs_alpha_weighted", round(cor(cophenetic(hc),cophenetic(hc_w)),3))
# the contested characters are a finding, not noise: record them with their alpha
contested <- per_df[!is.na(per_df$alpha) & per_df$alpha < .667, ]
contested <- contested[order(contested$alpha), ]
put("contested_characters", paste(sprintf("%s(%.2f)", contested$character, contested$alpha), collapse="; "))

```

## Freeze manifest

```{r manifest}
put("human_validation", "PENDING (kill-switch not yet evaluated; LLM-LLM reliability only so far)")
put("second_lens_embedding", "PENDING (character-free cross-check)")
w <- data.frame(metric=names(MAN), value=vapply(MAN, function(x) as.character(x[1]), character(1)))
write.csv(w, file.path(TAB,"E5_typologie_cisla.csv"), row.names=FALSE)
cat("Wrote E5_typologie_cisla.csv (",nrow(w),")\n",sep="")

# Complete the frozen manifest whenever the already-collected validation and
# embedding inputs are present. These scripts replace the two PENDING keys and
# append their metrics without clobbering the core coding results above.
if (file.exists(file.path(PROC, "validation_pilot_FILLED.csv")))
  source(file.path(ROOT, "analyzy", "scripts", "validate_pilot.R"), chdir=TRUE)
if (file.exists(file.path(PROC, "typology_embed_dist.csv")))
  source(file.path(ROOT, "analyzy", "scripts", "converge_typology.R"), chdir=TRUE)
```
